input.description: Explore unsloth codebase structure and save methods
input.prompt:
I need to understand the structure of the unsloth codebase, specifically how model saving and export methods are implemented. Please explore the following:

1. Find all Python files in the codebase and map out the directory structure
2. Find the main save/export module(s) - look for files with names like save.py, export.py, etc.
3. Find how GGUF export is implemented
4. Find how adapter saving (LoRA adapters) is implemented
5. Find how merged model saving is implemented
6. Look for any existing ONNX-related code

Please provide:
- The full directory tree
- The file paths of all save/export related files
- Key class names and method names found in those files
- How methods are registered/exposed (e.g., are they added to a class via monkey patching, inheritance, etc.)

Be thorough - read the key files completely if they're not too large.

28	class LoRA_MLP(torch.autograd.Function):
29	    """
30	    ### LoRA weights
31	    G = G + Ag @ Bg
32	    U = U + Au @ Bu
33	    W = W + Aw @ Bw
34	
35	    ### SwiGLU(X)
36	    e = X @ G
37	    f = e * sigmoid(e)
38	    g = X @ U
39	    h = f * g
40	    i = h @ W
41	
42	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
43	    See our blog post for more details
44	
45	    df = sigmoid(e) * (1 - f) + f
46	    dC/dW = h.T @ dY
47	    dC/dU = X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
48	    dC/dG = X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
49	
50	    ### Down projection LoRA weights
51	    dC/dAw = dC/dW @ B.T
52	    dC/dBw = A.T @ dC/dW
53	    dC/dAw =       h.T @ dY @ B.T
54	    dC/dBw = A.T @ h.T @ dY
55	
56	    ### Up projection LoRA weights
57	    dC/dAu =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * f) @ B.T
58	    dC/dBu = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * f)
59	
60	    ### Gate projection LoRA weights
61	    dC/dAg =       X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g) @ B.T
62	    dC/dBg = A.T @ X.T @ (D @ W.T * df * g)
63	
64	    Don't forget to see our blog post for more details!
65	    """
66	    @staticmethod
67	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
68	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
69	                gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
70	                  upW,   upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
71	                downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
72	                _forward_function, _backward_function,
73	                inplace = True,):
74	        dtype = X.dtype
75	
76	        e = matmul_lora(X, gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS)
77	        g = matmul_lora(X,   upW,   upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS)
78	        h = _forward_function(e, g)
79	        i = matmul_lora(h, downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS)
80	
81	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
82	            gateW, gateW_quant, gateS,
83	            upW, upW_quant, upS,
84	            downW, downW_quant, downS,
85	            _backward_function,
86	        )
87	        ctx.save_for_backward(gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB,
88	                              X, e, g)
89	        ctx.inplace = inplace
90	        return i
91	    pass
92	
93	
94	    @staticmethod
95	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
96	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
97	        gateW, gateW_quant, gateS, upW, upW_quant, upS, downW, downW_quant, downS, \
98	            _backward_function = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
99	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB, \
100	            X, e, g = ctx.saved_tensors
101	
102	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
103	        dY = dY.view(-1, dY.shape[-1])
104	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
105	        e  = e .view(-1, e .shape[-1])
106	        g  = g .view(-1, g .shape[-1])
107	        dtype = X.dtype
108	
109	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
110	            gateA.to(dtype), gateB.to(dtype), upA.to(dtype), upB.to(dtype), downA.to(dtype), downB.to(dtype)
111	
112	        gateA, gateB, upA, upB, downA, downB = \
113	            gateA.t(), gateB.t(), upA.t(), upB.t(), downA.t(), downB.t()
114	
115	        DW = matmul_lora(dY, downW.t(), downW_quant, downB, downA, downS)
116	        DW, e, g = _backward_function(DW, e, g)
117	        h, df, de = DW, e, g
118	
119	        d_downA = torch.empty_like(downA)
120	        d_downB = torch.empty_like(downB)
121	        d_gateA = torch.empty_like(gateA)
122	        d_gateB = torch.empty_like(gateB)
123	        d_upA   = torch.empty_like(upA)
124	        d_upB   = torch.empty_like(upB)
125	
126	        # Down projection LoRA weights
127	        # d_downA = h.t() @ (dY @ downB.t())
128	        # d_downB = (downA.t() @ h.t()) @ dY
129	        # d_downA *= downS
130	        # d_downB *= downS
131	        d_downA.addmm_(h.t(), dY @ downB.t(), alpha = downS, beta = 0)
132	        d_downB.addmm_(downA.t() @ h.t(), dY, alpha = downS, beta = 0)
133	
134	        # Up projection LoRA weights
135	        # d_upA   = X.t() @ (df @ upB.t())
136	        # d_upB   = (upA.t() @ X.t()) @ df
137	        # d_upA  *= upS
138	        # d_upB  *= upS
139	        d_upA.addmm_(X.t(), df @ upB.t(), alpha = upS, beta = 0)
140	        d_upB.addmm_(upA.t() @ X.t(), df, alpha = upS, beta = 0)
141	
142	        # Gate projection LoRA weights
143	        # d_gateA = X.t() @ (de @ gateB.t())
144	        # d_gateB = (gateA.t() @ X.t()) @ de
145	        # d_gateA *= gateS
146	        # d_gateB *= gateS
147	        d_gateA.addmm_(X.t(), de @ gateB.t(), alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
148	        d_gateB.addmm_(gateA.t() @ X.t(), de, alpha = gateS, beta = 0)
149	
150	        # dX  = matmul_lora(df, upW.t(), upW_quant, upB, upA, upS)
151	        # dX += matmul_lora(de, gateW.t(), gateW_quant, gateB, gateA, gateS)
152	        upW = fast_dequantize(upW.t(), upW_quant)
153	        dX = torch.matmul(df, upW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
154	        del upW
155	        # dX += df @ upB.to(dtype).t() @ (upS * upA.to(dtype).t())
156	        dX.addmm_(df @ upB.t(), upA.t(), alpha = upS)
157	
158	        gateW = fast_dequantize(gateW.t(), gateW_quant)
159	        # dX += de @ gateW.t()
160	        dX.addmm_(de, gateW.t())
161	        del gateW
162	        # dX += de @ gateB.to(dtype).t() @ (gateS * gateA.to(dtype).t())
163	        dX.addmm_(de @ gateB.t(), gateA.t(), alpha = gateS)
164	
165	        # gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
166	        #  upW,    upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS,
167	        # downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
168	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
169	            None, None, d_gateA.t(), d_gateB.t(), None, \
170	            None, None,   d_upA.t(),   d_upB.t(), None, \
171	            None, None, d_downA.t(), d_downB.t(), None, \
172	            None, None, None, # _backward and _forward and inplace
173	    pass
174	pass

178	def apply_lora_mlp_swiglu(self, X, inplace = True):
179	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.gate_proj)
180	    gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS = get_lora_parameters(self.gate_proj)
181	    upW,     upW_quant,   upA,   upB,   upS = get_lora_parameters(self.  up_proj)
182	    downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS = get_lora_parameters(self.down_proj)
183	    out = LoRA_MLP.apply(X,
184	                         gateW, gateW_quant, gateA, gateB, gateS,
185	                         upW,     upW_quant, upA,   upB,   upS,
186	                         downW, downW_quant, downA, downB, downS,
187	                         swiglu_fg_kernel, swiglu_DWf_DW_dfg_kernel,
188	                         inplace,)
189	    return out

224	class LoRA_QKV(torch.autograd.Function):
225	    """
226	    ### LoRA weights
227	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
228	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
229	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
230	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
231	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
232	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
233	
234	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
235	    See our blogpost for more details.
236	
237	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
238	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
239	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
240	    We then sum them all find dC/dX
241	
242	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
243	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
244	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
245	
246	    ### K projection LoRA weights
247	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
248	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
249	
250	    ### V projection LoRA weights
251	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
252	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
253	    """
254	    @staticmethod
255	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
256	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
257	                QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
258	                KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
259	                VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
260	                inplace = True):
261	        dtype = X.dtype
262	
263	        Q = matmul_lora(X, QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS)
264	        K = matmul_lora(X, KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS)
265	        V = matmul_lora(X, VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS)
266	
267	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (
268	            QW, QW_quant, QS,
269	            KW, KW_quant, KS,
270	            VW, VW_quant, VS,
271	        )
272	        ctx.save_for_backward(X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB,)
273	        ctx.inplace = inplace
274	        return Q, K, V
275	    pass
276	
277	    @staticmethod
278	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
279	    def backward(ctx, dQ, dK, dV):
280	        QW, QW_quant, QS, KW, KW_quant, KS, VW, VW_quant, VS = \
281	            ctx.custom_saved_tensors
282	        X, QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB, = ctx.saved_tensors
283	
284	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
285	        dQ = dQ.view(-1, dQ.shape[-1])
286	        dK = dK.reshape(-1, dK.shape[-1]) # view doesn't work on K.T
287	        dV = dV.view(-1, dV.shape[-1])
288	        X  = X .view(-1, X .shape[-1])
289	        dtype = X.dtype
290	
291	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
292	            QA.to(dtype), QB.to(dtype), KA.to(dtype), KB.to(dtype), VA.to(dtype), VB.to(dtype)
293	
294	        QA, QB, KA, KB, VA, VB = \
295	            QA.t(), QB.t(), KA.t(), KB.t(), VA.t(), VB.t()
296	
297	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
298	        # See our blogpost for more details.
299	        d_QA = torch.empty_like(QA)
300	        d_QB = torch.empty_like(QB)
301	        d_KA = torch.empty_like(KA)
302	        d_KB = torch.empty_like(KB)
303	        d_VA = torch.empty_like(VA)
304	        d_VB = torch.empty_like(VB)
305	
306	        # Q Projection
307	        # d_QA = X.t() @ (dQ @ QB.t())
308	        # d_QB = (QA.t() @ X.t()) @ dQ
309	        # d_QA *= QS
310	        # d_QB *= QS
311	        d_QA.addmm_(X.t(), dQ @ QB.t(), alpha = QS, beta = 0)
312	        d_QB.addmm_(QA.t() @ X.t(), dQ, alpha = QS, beta = 0)
313	
314	        # K Projection
315	        # d_KA = X.t() @ (dK @ KB.t())
316	        # d_KB = (KA.t() @ X.t()) @ dK
317	        # d_KA *= KS
318	        # d_KB *= KS
319	        d_KA.addmm_(X.t(), dK @ KB.t(), alpha = KS, beta = 0)
320	        d_KB.addmm_(KA.t() @ X.t(), dK, alpha = KS, beta = 0)
321	
322	        # V Projection
323	        # d_VA = X.t() @ (dV @ VB.t())
324	        # d_VB = (VA.t() @ X.t()) @ dV
325	        # d_VA *= VS
326	        # d_VB *= VS
327	        d_VA.addmm_(X.t(), dV @ VB.t(), alpha = VS, beta = 0)
328	        d_VB.addmm_(VA.t() @ X.t(), dV, alpha = VS, beta = 0)
329	
330	        # Combine derivatives to find dX
331	        # dQ
332	        QW = fast_dequantize(QW.t(), QW_quant)
333	        dX = torch.matmul(dQ, QW.t(), out = X if ctx.inplace else None)
334	        del QW
335	        # dX += (dQ @ QB.to(dtype).t() @ (QS * QA.to(dtype).t()))
336	        dX.addmm_(dQ @ QB.t(), QA.t(), alpha = QS)
337	
338	        # dK
339	        KW = fast_dequantize(KW.t(), KW_quant)
340	        # dX += dK @ KW.t()
341	        dX.addmm_(dK, KW.t())
342	        del KW
343	        # dX += dK @ KB.to(dtype).t() @ (KS * KA.to(dtype).t())
344	        dX.addmm_(dK @ KB.t(), KA.t(), alpha = KS)
345	
346	        # dV
347	        VW = fast_dequantize(VW.t(), VW_quant)
348	        # dX += dV @ VW.t()
349	        dX.addmm_(dV, VW.t())
350	        del VW
351	        # dX += dV @ VB.to(dtype).t() @ (VS * VA.to(dtype).t())
352	        dX.addmm_(dV @ VB.t(), VA.t(), alpha = VS)
353	
354	        # QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
355	        # KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
356	        # VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
357	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
358	            None, None, d_QA.t(), d_QB.t(), None, \
359	            None, None, d_KA.t(), d_KB.t(), None, \
360	            None, None, d_VA.t(), d_VB.t(), None, \
361	            None,
362	    pass

366	def apply_lora_qkv(self, X, inplace = True):
367	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.q_proj)
368	    QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS = get_lora_parameters(self.q_proj)
369	    KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS = get_lora_parameters(self.k_proj)
370	    VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS = get_lora_parameters(self.v_proj)
371	    Q, K, V = LoRA_QKV.apply(X,
372	        QW, QW_quant, QA, QB, QS,
373	        KW, KW_quant, KA, KB, KS,
374	        VW, VW_quant, VA, VB, VS,
375	        inplace,
376	    )
377	    return Q, K, V

381	class LoRA_W(torch.autograd.Function):
382	    """
383	    ### LoRA weights
384	    Wq = Wq + Aq @ Bq
385	    Wk = Wk + Ak @ Bk
386	    Wv = Wv + Av @ Bv
387	    Q = X @ Wq = X @ Wq + X @ Aq @ Bq
388	    K = X @ Wk = X @ Wk + X @ Ak @ Bk
389	    V = X @ Wv = X @ Wv + X @ Av @ Bv
390	
391	    ### Backpropagation chain rule
392	    dC/dWq = X.T @ D(Wq)
393	    dC/dWk = X.T @ D(Wk)
394	    dC/dWv = X.T @ D(Wv)
395	
396	    ### Q projection LoRA weights
397	    dC/dAq =       X.T @ D(Wq) @ B.T
398	    dC/dBq = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wq)
399	
400	    ### K projection LoRA weights
401	    dC/dAk =       X.T @ D(Wk) @ B.T
402	    dC/dBk = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wk)
403	
404	    ### V projection LoRA weights
405	    dC/dAv =       X.T @ D(Wv) @ B.T
406	    dC/dBv = A.T @ X.T @ D(Wv)
407	    """
408	    @staticmethod
409	    @torch_amp_custom_fwd
410	    def forward(ctx, X : torch.Tensor,
411	                W, W_quant, A, B, S):
412	        dtype = X.dtype
413	        XW = matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, A, B, S)
414	        ctx.custom_saved_tensors = (W, W_quant, S,)
415	        ctx.save_for_backward(A, B, X)
416	        return XW
417	    pass
418	
419	    @staticmethod
420	    @torch_amp_custom_bwd
421	    def backward(ctx, dY : torch.Tensor):
422	        W, W_quant, S = ctx.custom_saved_tensors
423	        A, B, X = ctx.saved_tensors
424	
425	        batch, seq_len, hd = X.shape
426	        dY = dY.reshape(-1, dY.shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
427	        X  = X .reshape(-1, X .shape[-1]) # Must be reshape
428	        dtype = X.dtype
429	
430	        A, B = A.to(dtype), B.to(dtype)
431	
432	        A, B = A.t(), B.t()
433	
434	        d_A = torch.empty_like(A)
435	        d_B = torch.empty_like(B)
436	
437	        ### Weight projection LoRA weights
438	        # Weight projection
439	        # d_A = X.t() @ (dY @ B.t())
440	        # d_B = (A.t() @ X.t()) @ dY
441	        # d_A *= S
442	        # d_B *= S
443	        d_A.addmm_(X.t(), dY @ B.t(), alpha = S, beta = 0)
444	        d_B.addmm_(A.t() @ X.t(), dY, alpha = S, beta = 0)
445	
446	        # Get derivative for dX
447	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant)
448	        dX = dY @ W.t()
449	        del W
450	        # dX += dY @ B.to(dtype).t() @ (S * A.to(dtype).t())
451	        dX.addmm_(dY @ B.t(), A.t(), alpha = S)
452	
453	        # W, W_quant, A, B, S
454	        return dX.view(batch, seq_len, hd), \
455	            None, None, d_A.t(), d_B.t(), None
456	    pass
457	pass

460	def apply_lora_o(self, X):
461	    X = _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X, self.o_proj)
462	    OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS = get_lora_parameters(self.o_proj)
463	    O = LoRA_W.apply(X, OW, OW_quant, OA, OB, OS)
464	    return O
465	pass

469	@torch._disable_dynamo
470	def fast_lora_forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.Tensor:
471	    raise NotImplementedError(
472	        "Unsloth: Currently not supported yet - reshaping done incorrectly"
473	    )
474	    self._check_forward_args(x, *args, **kwargs)
475	    adapter_names = kwargs.pop("adapter_names", None)
476	
477	    if self.disable_adapters:
478	        if self.merged:
479	            self.unmerge()
480	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
481	    elif adapter_names is not None:
482	        result = self._mixed_batch_forward(x, *args, adapter_names=adapter_names, **kwargs)
483	    elif self.merged:
484	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
485	    else:
486	        # Fastpath
487	        if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
488	            active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
489	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys(): return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
490	
491	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
492	            if isinstance(dropout, IDENTITY_DROPOUT) and not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
493	                lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
494	                lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
495	                scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
496	                W = self.base_layer.weight
497	                return LoRA_W.apply(x, W, QUANT_STATE(W), lora_A, lora_B, scaling)
498	            pass
499	        pass
500	
501	        result = self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
502	        # As per Tim Dettmers, for 4bit, we need to defensively clone here.
503	        # The reason is that in some cases, an error can occur that backprop
504	        # does not work on a manipulated view. This issue may be solved with
505	        # newer PyTorch versions but this would need extensive testing to be
506	        # sure.
507	        result = result.clone()
508	
509	        for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
510	            if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
511	                continue
512	            lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
513	            lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
514	            dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
515	            scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
516	
517	            requires_conversion = not torch.is_autocast_enabled()
518	            if requires_conversion:
519	                expected_dtype = result.dtype
520	                x = x.to(lora_A.weight.dtype)
521	
522	            if not self.use_dora[active_adapter]:
523	                result = result + lora_B(lora_A(dropout(x))) * scaling
524	            else:
525	                if isinstance(dropout, torch.nn.Identity) or not self.training:
526	                    base_result = result
527	                else:
528	                    x = dropout(x)
529	                    base_result = None
530	
531	                result = result + self.lora_magnitude_vector[active_adapter](
532	                    x,
533	                    lora_A=lora_A,
534	                    lora_B=lora_B,
535	                    scaling=scaling,
536	                    base_layer=self.get_base_layer(),
537	                    base_result=base_result,
538	                )
539	            if requires_conversion:
540	                result = result.to(expected_dtype)
541	
542	    return result

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X
271	pass

274	# INTEL GPU Specific Logic
275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset
339	
340	            # Dequantize W
341	            fx = cdequantize_blockwise_fp16_nf4 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
342	                 cdequantize_blockwise_bf16_nf4
343	            fx(get_ptr(None), get_ptr(W), ptr_out_absmax, get_ptr(out),
344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)
345	        pass
346	        # Careful returning transposed data
347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)
348	        return out.t() if is_transposed else out
349	    pass
350	# NVIDIA GPU Default Logic

354	        if quant_state is None: return W
355	        if type(quant_state) is not list:

369	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
370	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
371	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
372	        pass

391	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
392

952	def save_to_gguf(
953	    model_type           : str,
954	    model_dtype          : str,
955	    is_sentencepiece     : bool = False,
956	    model_directory      : str = "unsloth_finetuned_model",
957	    quantization_method  = "fast_quantized", # Can be a list of options! ["q4_k_m", "q8_0", "q5_k_m"]
958	    first_conversion     : str = None,
959	    _run_installer = None, # Non blocking install of llama.cpp
960	):
961	    # logger.warning(
962	    #     "NOTICE: llama.cpp GGUF conversion is currently unstable, since llama.cpp is\n"\
963	    #     "undergoing some major bug fixes as at 5th of May 2024. This is not an Unsloth issue.\n"\
964	    #     "Please be patient - GGUF saving should still work, but might not work as well."
965	    # )
966	    assert(model_dtype == "float16" or model_dtype == "bfloat16")
967	    model_dtype = "f16" if model_dtype == "float16" else "bf16"
968	
969	    # Convert quantization_method to list
970	    if   isinstance(quantization_method, list):  pass
971	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, str):   quantization_method = [ quantization_method, ]
972	    elif isinstance(quantization_method, tuple): quantization_method = list(quantization_method)
973	    else:
974	        raise TypeError("Unsloth: quantization_method can only be a string or a list of strings")
975	    pass
976	
977	    # Check if bfloat16 is supported
978	    if model_dtype == "bf16" and not torch.cuda.is_bf16_supported():
979	        logger.warning(
980	            "Unsloth: Cannot convert to bf16 GGUF since your computer doesn't support it.\n"\
981	            "We shall switch instead to f16."
982	        )
983	        model_dtype = "f16"
984	    pass
985	
986	    # Check first_conversion as well
987	    if first_conversion is None:
988	        first_conversion = model_dtype
989	    pass
990	
991	    # Check I quants
992	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
993	        if quant_method.startswith("iq2"):
994	            raise RuntimeError("Unsloth: Currently iq2 type quantizations aren't supported yet - sorry!")
995	    pass
996	
997	    # Careful convert.py is only for Llama / Mistral based archs
998	    use_fast_convert = False
999	    if not is_sentencepiece:      use_fast_convert = False # Llama-3
1000	    elif model_type == "llama":   use_fast_convert = True
1001	    elif model_type == "mistral": use_fast_convert = True
1002	    pass
1003	    logger.warning_once(f"Unsloth: Converting {model_type} model. Can use fast conversion = {use_fast_convert}.")
1004	
1005	    # Map quant methods
1006	    new_quantization_method = []
1007	    for quant_method in quantization_method:
1008	        if   quant_method == "not_quantized":  quant_method = model_dtype
1009	        elif quant_method == "fast_quantized": quant_method = "q8_0"
1010	        elif quant_method == "quantized":      quant_method = "q4_k_m"
1011	        elif quant_method is None:             quant_method = "q8_0"
1012	
1013	        # Check if wrong method
1014	        if quant_method not in ALLOWED_QUANTS.keys():
1015	            error = f"Unsloth: Quant method = [{quant_method}] not supported. Choose from below:\n"
1016	            for key, value in ALLOWED_QUANTS.items():
1017	                error += f"[{key}] => {value}\n"
1018	            raise RuntimeError(error)
1019	        pass
1020	
1021	        new_quantization_method.append(quant_method)
1022	    pass
1023	    quantization_method = new_quantization_method
1024	
1025	    print_info = \
1026	        f"==((====))==  Unsloth: Conversion from QLoRA to GGUF information\n"\
1027	        f"   {chr(92)}{chr(92)}   /|    [0] Installing llama.cpp might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1028	        f"O^O/ {chr(92)}_/ {chr(92)}    [1] Converting HF to GGUF 16bits might take 3 minutes.\n"\
1029	        f"{chr(92)}        /    [2] Converting GGUF 16bits to {quantization_method} might take 10 minutes each.\n"\
1030	        f' "-____-"     In total, you will have to wait at least 16 minutes.\n'
1031	    print(print_info)
1032	
1033	    # Check first_conversion format
1034	    if   first_conversion == "f16"  : pass
1035	    elif first_conversion == "bf16" : pass
1036	    elif first_conversion == "f32"  : pass
1037	    elif first_conversion == "q8_0" : pass
1038	    else:
1039	        raise RuntimeError(
1040	            f"Unsloth: `first_conversion` can only be one of ['f16', 'bf16', 'f32', 'q8_0'] and not `{first_conversion}`."
1041	        )
1042	    pass
1043	
1044	    # Determine whether the system already has llama.cpp installed and the scripts are executable
1045	    quantize_location = get_executable(["llama-quantize", "quantize", "llama-quantize.exe", "quantize.exe"])
1046	    convert_location  = get_executable(["convert-hf-to-gguf.py", "convert_hf_to_gguf.py"])
1047	
1048	    error = 0
1049	    if quantize_location is not None and convert_location is not None:
1050	        print("Unsloth: llama.cpp found in the system. We shall skip installation.")
1051	    else:
1052	        print("Unsloth: Installing llama.cpp. This might take 3 minutes...")
1053	        if _run_installer is not None:
1054	            _run_installer, IS_CMAKE = _run_installer
1055	
1056	            error = _run_installer.wait()
1057	            # Check if successful
1058	            if error != 0:
1059	                print(f"Unsloth: llama.cpp error code = {error}.")
1060	                install_llama_cpp_old(-10)
1061	            pass
1062	
1063	            if IS_CMAKE:
1064	                # CMAKE needs to do some extra steps
1065	                print("Unsloth: CMAKE detected. Finalizing some steps for installation.")
1066	
1067	                check = os.system("cp llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-* llama.cpp")
1068	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1069	                check = os.system("rm -rf llama.cpp/build")
1070	                if check != 0: raise RuntimeError("Failed compiling llama.cpp. Please report this ASAP!")
1071	            pass
1072	        else:
1073	            error = 0
1074	            install_llama_cpp_blocking()
1075	        pass
1076	
1077	        # Careful llama.cpp/quantize changed to llama.cpp/llama-quantize
1078	        # and llama.cpp/main changed to llama.cpp/llama-cli
1079	        # See https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp/pull/7809
1080	        quantize_location = None
1081	        if os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize.exe"):
1082	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize.exe"
1083	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/quantize"):
1084	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/quantize"
1085	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"):
1086	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize.exe"
1087	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/llama-quantize"):
1088	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/llama-quantize"
1089	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"):
1090	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/llama-quantize"
1091	        elif os.path.exists("llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"):
1092	            quantize_location = "llama.cpp/build/bin/quantize"
1093	        else:
1094	            raise RuntimeError(
1095	                "Unsloth: The file 'llama.cpp/llama-quantize' or `llama.cpp/quantize` does not exist.\n"\
1096	                "We've also double checked the building directory under 'llama.cpp/build/bin/'.\n"\
1097	                "But we expect this file to exist! Check if the file exists under llama.cpp and investigate the building process of llama.cpp (make/cmake)!"
1098	            )
1099	        pass
1100

137	def _free_cached_model(model):
138	    from huggingface_hub import scan_cache_dir
139	    cached_repos = list(scan_cache_dir().repos)
140	
141	    # Go through every cached repo, and delete the one that matches the model we want to save.
142	    # Can save 4GB of disk space - useful for Kaggle systems.
143	    for cached_repo in cached_repos:
144	        if cached_repo.repo_id == model.config._name_or_path:
145	            remove_cache_commit = list(cached_repo.revisions)[0].commit_hash
146	            delete_strategy = scan_cache_dir().delete_revisions(remove_cache_commit,)
147	
148	            logger.warning_once(
149	                "Unsloth: Will remove a cached repo with size " + \
150	                delete_strategy.expected_freed_size_str,
151	            )
152	
153	            delete_strategy.execute()
154	        pass
155	    pass

159	def _merge_lora(layer, name):
160	
161	    bias = getattr(layer, "bias", None)
162	    if isinstance(layer, (Bnb_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear4bit, Peft_Linear)):
163	        # Is LoRA so we need to merge!
164	        W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
165	        if quant_state is not None:
166	            dtype = quant_state.dtype if type(quant_state) is not list else quant_state[2]
167	            W = fast_dequantize(W, quant_state)
168	        else:
169	            dtype = W.dtype
170	        W = W.to(torch.float32).t()
171	        # W = W.t()
172	
173	        if A is not None:
174	            # sAB = (A.t().to(torch.float32) @ (s * B.t().to(torch.float32)))
175	            # W += sAB
176	            W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
177	            # W.addmm_(A.t().to(W.dtype), B.t().to(W.dtype), alpha = s)
178	            # if not torch.isfinite(W).all():
179	            maximum_element = torch.max(W.min().abs(), W.max())
180	            if not torch.isfinite(maximum_element).item():
181	                raise ValueError(f"Unsloth: Merge failed.\n{name} has some elements = infinity.")
182	        pass
183	        W = W.t().to(dtype)
184	    else:
185	        W = layer.weight
186	    return W, bias
187	pass

2449	    del arguments["self"]

2451	    for _ in range(3):
2452	        gc.collect()

2504	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
2505	    tokenizer            = None,
2506	    torchao_config       = None,
2507	    push_to_hub          : bool = False,
2508	    token                : Optional[Union[str, bool]] = None,
2509	):
2510	    """Quantizes the model with torchao and saves a torchao quantized checkpoint
2511	
2512	    Args
2513	      `save_directory`: local folder path or huggingface hub ID when `push_to_hub` is set to True, e.g. `my_model`
2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize
2515	      `push_to_hub` (bool): whether to push the checkpoint to huggingface hub or save locally
2516	    """
2517	    # first merge the lora weights
2518	    arguments = dict(locals())
2519	    arguments["model"]        = self
2520	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
2521	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
2522	    arguments["save_method"]  = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
2523	    del arguments["self"]
2524	    del arguments["torchao_config"]
2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)
2526	    for _ in range(3):
2527	        gc.collect()
2528	
2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:
2532	        from torchao.quantization import Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig
2533	        torchao_config = Int8DynamicActivationInt8WeightConfig()
2534	    quantization_config = TorchAoConfig(quant_type = torchao_config)
2535	
2536	    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(arguments["save_directory"])
2537	    if HAS_TORCH_DTYPE:
2538	        kwargs = {"torch_dtype" : "auto"}
2539	    else:
2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
2542	        arguments["save_directory"],
2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549	
2550	    if push_to_hub:
2551	        if token is None and push_to_hub: token = get_token()
2552	        # torchao does not support safe_serialization right now
2553	        model.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization = False, token = token)
2554	        tokenizer.push_to_hub(torchao_save_directory, token = token)
2555	    else:
2556	        model.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory, safe_serialization=False)
2557	        tokenizer.save_pretrained(torchao_save_directory)
2558	    pass
2559	    for _ in range(3):
2560	        gc.collect()
2561	pass

2565	    raise NotImplementedError("Unsloth: Sorry GGUF is currently not supported for vision models!")

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information about the LoRA adapter structure in unsloth. Let me compile the final report:

## Summary: LoRA Adapter Structure in Unsloth Codebase

Based on my analysis of the unsloth codebase, here's a comprehensive overview of how LoRA adapters are structured:

### 1. **All LoRA-Related Files**

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` - Core LoRA inference kernels
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/kernels/utils.py` - Utility functions for accessing LoRA parameters
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/save.py` - LoRA weight merging and saving
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/models/llama.py` - LLaMA model LoRA configuration
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/models/vision.py` - FastBaseModel LoRA setup
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775329277479-6t5hli/unsloth/models/_utils.py` - PEFT integration utilities
- Test files: `tests/qlora/test_hf_qlora_train_and_merge.py`, `tests/qlora/test_unsloth_qlora_train_and_merge.py`

### 2. **How LoRA A and B Matrices are Accessed**

**In `unsloth/kernels/utils.py` (lines 190-257):**

LoRA weights are accessed via the `get_lora_parameters()` and `get_lora_parameters_bias()` functions:

```python
def get_lora_parameters(proj):
    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
    adapter = adapter[0]
    
    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]  # Dictionary access by adapter name
    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]  # Dictionary access by adapter name
    A = lora_A_linear.weight  # Actual weight tensor
    B = lora_B_linear.weight  # Actual weight tensor
    
    return (W, quant_state, A, B, proj.scaling[adapter])
```

**Key access patterns:**
- `proj.lora_A[adapter]` - Returns a Linear layer containing the LoRA A matrix
- `proj.lora_A[adapter].weight` - The actual weight tensor (in_features × rank)
- `proj.lora_B[adapter]` - Returns a Linear layer containing the LoRA B matrix
- `proj.lora_B[adapter].weight` - The actual weight tensor (rank × out_features)
- `proj.scaling[adapter]` - Scaling factor (lora_alpha / rank)

### 3. **How Rank and Alpha are Stored**

**Rank and Alpha access:**
- **Stored in PEFT config:** `model.peft_config["adapter_name"].r` and `model.peft_config["adapter_name"].lora_alpha`
- **Computed scaling:** `proj.scaling[adapter] = lora_alpha / r`

**In `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2334-2346):**

The config is accessed via:
```python
peft_config = model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()
peft_config["r"]
peft_config["lora_alpha"]
peft_config["lora_dropout"]
```

Also accessed directly at lines 2811-2812:
```python
lora_dropout = model.peft_config[active_adapter].lora_dropout
```

### 4. **Target Modules Configuration**

**Default target_modules in `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2269-2270):**

```python
target_modules = ["q_proj", "k_proj", "v_proj", "o_proj",
                  "gate_proj", "up_proj", "down_proj"]
```

**Accessed and validated in lines 2346-2360:**
```python
old_target_modules = list(peft_config["target_modules"])
modules_to_save = peft_config["modules_to_save"]
```

Target modules can also include:
- "embed_tokens"
- "lm_head"
- Any custom modules defined via `modules_to_save` parameter

### 5. **Model Adapter Config Storage and Access**

**Configuration is stored in:** `model.peft_config` - A dictionary where:
- Keys are adapter names (typically "default")
- Values are `LoraConfig` objects

**Key attributes accessed:**
- `model.peft_config.keys()` - Get all adapter names
- `model.peft_config["default"]` - Get specific adapter config
- `model.peft_config["default"].to_dict()` - Convert to dictionary
- `model.peft_config[adapter_name].loftq_config` - LoFTQ configuration

**Active adapters accessed at:**
```python
active_adapter = model.active_adapters[0] if hasattr(model, "active_adapters") else model.active_adapter
```

### 6. **Existing Patterns for Iterating Over LoRA Layers**

**In `unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` (lines 487-542):**

Pattern for iterating over multiple adapters:
```python
# Single adapter fastpath
if len(self.active_adapters) == 1:
    active_adapter = self.active_adapters[0]
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        return self.base_layer(x, *args, **kwargs)
    
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter].weight
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter].weight
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]

# Multiple adapters - iterate
for active_adapter in self.active_adapters:
    if active_adapter not in self.lora_A.keys():
        continue
    lora_A = self.lora_A[active_adapter]
    lora_B = self.lora_B[active_adapter]
    dropout = self.lora_dropout[active_adapter]
    scaling = self.scaling[active_adapter]
```

**In `unsloth/models/llama.py` (lines 2772-2783):**

Iterating over peft_config:
```python
for active_adapter in model.peft_config.keys():
    name = model.peft_config[active_adapter].base_model_name_or_path
    # Process each adapter
```

### 7. **LoRA Weight Merging Pattern**

**In `unsloth/save.py` (lines 159-187):**

The `_merge_lora()` function demonstrates the complete merging:
```python
def _merge_lora(layer, name):
    W, quant_state, A, B, s, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(layer)
    
    if A is not None:
        # W += s * A.T @ B.T
        W.addmm_(A.t().to(torch.float32), B.t().to(torch.float32), alpha = s)
```

This shows the mathematical operation: **Output = W + (alpha/rank) × A.T @ B.T**

### 8. **LoRA Configuration Creation Pattern**

**In `unsloth/models/vision.py` (lines 888-899):**

```python
local_variables = { **locals(), **kwargs, }
allowed_parameters = inspect.signature(LoraConfig).parameters.keys()
lora_config = LoraConfig(
    **{ k : v for k, v in local_variables.items() if k in allowed_parameters },
)
model = _get_peft_model(model, lora_config)
```

This pattern ensures only valid PEFT LoraConfig parameters are passed during initialization.

---

All LoRA operations in unsloth are built on top of PEFT (Parameter Efficient Fine-Tuning) library and follow the standard PEFT LoRA interface with optimized kernels for fast inference.

## Unsloth Codebase Structure & Save/Export Implementation Analysis

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

The unsloth codebase is organized as follows:

```
unsloth/ (Main package)
├── Core Files
│   ├── __init__.py (Package initialization - imports from save.py)
│   ├── save.py (2678 lines - PRIMARY SAVE/EXPORT IMPLEMENTATION)

│   ├── loader_utils.py (get_model_name utility)

│

│

│   └── Models registered by architecture type
│
├── dataprep/ (Data preparation)
│   └── synthetic.py, synthetic_configs.py
│
└── utils/
    └── hf_hub.py (HuggingFace Hub utilities)
```

---

### 2. SAVE/EXPORT MODULE - PRIMARY FILE ANALYSIS

**Location:** `/unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)

#### **Key Imports & Dependencies:**
```python
from unsloth_zoo.utils import Version, dtype_from_config
from unsloth_zoo.hf_utils import HAS_TORCH_DTYPE
from bitsandbytes.nn import Linear4bit as Bnb_Linear4bit
from peft.tuners.lora import Linear4bit as Peft_Linear4bit, Linear as Peft_Linear
from .kernels import fast_dequantize, QUANT_STATE, get_lora_parameters_bias
from .tokenizer_utils import fix_sentencepiece_gguf
from huggingface_hub import HfApi, get_token
from unsloth_zoo.saving_utils import merge_and_overwrite_lora, prepare_saving
from unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp import install_llama_cpp, convert_to_gguf
```

---

### 3. MAIN SAVE/EXPORT FUNCTIONS

#### **A. Core Save Function**

**`unsloth_save_model()` (Lines 205-763)**
- Primary function for all save operations
- Parameters:
  - `save_method`: "lora" | "merged_16bit" | "merged_4bit" | "merged_4bit_forced"
  - `push_to_hub`: Boolean for HuggingFace Hub upload
  - `maximum_memory_usage`: Float (0-0.95) for memory management
- Handles:
  - LoRA adapter saving
  - Merged model saving (16bit and 4bit)
  - Tokenizer saving (with padding_side adjustment to "left")
  - Automatic tag addition (["unsloth"])
  - Push to HuggingFace Hub integration

---

#### **B. GGUF Export Functions**

**`save_to_gguf()` (Lines 952-1320)**
- Converts models to GGUF format via llama.cpp
- Key parameters:
  - `model_type`: str (llama, mistral, etc.)
  - `model_dtype`: "float16" or "bfloat16"
  - `is_sentencepiece`: bool (for tokenizer compatibility)
  - `quantization_method`: str or list (supports multiple quants)
  - `first_conversion`: Initial conversion format
- Supported quantization methods:
  - `f32`, `bf16`, `f16` (no quantization)
  - `q8_0` (fast_quantized)
  - `q4_k_m`, `q5_k_m`, `q4_k_s`, `q5_k_s`, `q6_k` (recommended)
  - `q4_0`, `q4_1`, `q5_0`, `q5_1` (standard)
  - `q2_k`, `q3_k_l`, `q3_k_m`, `q3_k_s`, `q3_k_xs`
- Handles llama.cpp installation (non-blocking for performance)
- Uses `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` for HF to GGUF conversion

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf()` (Lines 1768-1950)**
- Instance method attached to models via monkey patching
- Wraps `save_to_gguf()` with merged 16-bit conversion
- Creates Ollama Modelfile automatically
- Parameters:
  - Same as `save_to_gguf()` but with additional model-specific handling
  - `first_conversion`: Optional initial conversion format

**`save_to_gguf_generic()` (Lines 2276-2355)**
- Vision model-specific GGUF export
- Uses simplified quantization approach
- Maps to `_convert_to_gguf` from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp`

---

#### **C. Merged Model Saving**

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_merged()` (Lines 1321-1361)**
- Instance method for saving merged models
- Delegates to `unsloth_save_model()` with model reference as `self`
- Uses `_merge_lora()` internally to merge LoRA weights with base weights

**`unsloth_push_to_hub_merged()` (Lines 1364-1407)**
- Push merged models to HuggingFace Hub
- Internally calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `push_to_hub=True`

**`_merge_lora()` (Lines 159-187)**
- Core LoRA merging implementation
- Handles:
  - Quantized weights (via `fast_dequantize()`)
  - Non-quantized weights
  - Bias terms
- Performs: `W = W.t() + alpha * A.t() @ B.t()` (LoRA equation)

---

#### **D. LoRA/Adapter Saving**

**`save_lora_to_custom_dir()` (Lines 2129-2140)**
- Saves LoRA adapters to custom directory
- Calls `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="lora"`

**`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally()` (Lines 2211-2262)**
- Converts LoRA adapters to GGML format
- Uses `llama.cpp/convert-lora-to-ggml.py`
- Output: `ggml-adapter-model.bin`

**`unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub()` (Lines 2143-2210)**
- Same as above but uploads to HuggingFace Hub
- Uses `upload_to_huggingface()` wrapper

---

#### **E. TorchAO Quantization**

**`unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao()` (Lines 2502-2561)**
- Saves model with PyTorch-AO quantization
- Flow:
  1. Merges LoRA to 16-bit
  2. Loads merged model with `TorchAoConfig`
  3. Applies quantization via `torchao.quantize_()`
  4. Saves to `{save_directory}-torchao`
- Supports custom `torchao_config` parameter
- Supports both local save and `push_to_hub`

---

#### **F. Generic Save Methods (for non-standard models)**

**`unsloth_generic_save()` (Lines 2356-2410)**
- Generic save for models that don't follow standard architecture
- Uses `merge_and_overwrite_lora()` from `unsloth_zoo`
- Delegates to external library for LoRA merging

**`unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged()` (Lines 2413-2453)**
- Instance method wrapper for generic save

**`unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged()` (Lines 2456-2501)**
- Push merged generic models to HuggingFace Hub

---

### 4. METHOD REGISTRATION & EXPOSURE

#### **Via `patch_saving_functions()` (Lines 2569-2677)**

Methods are added to model instances using **monkey patching with `types.MethodType`**:

```python
model.save_pretrained_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_save_pretrained_merged, model)
model.push_to_hub_merged = types.MethodType(unsloth_generic_push_to_hub_merged, model)
model.save_pretrained_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf, model)
model.push_to_hub_gguf = types.MethodType(unsloth_push_to_hub_gguf, model)
model.save_pretrained_torchao = types.MethodType(unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao, model)
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
```

**Additionally:**
- Original `push_to_hub()` is wrapped with custom `unsloth_push_to_hub()` via exec()
- Stores original as `model.original_push_to_hub`
- Adds model tags automatically: `["unsloth"]`

#### **Initialization**

Called during model loading in `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` and `FastModel.from_pretrained()` (from `models/loader.py`)

---

### 5. GGUF IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

#### **Quantization Constants** (Lines 79-107)
Defined dictionary `ALLOWED_QUANTS` with 20+ quantization methods

#### **Installation Flow**

Non-blocking installation functions:
- `install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()` - Git clone
- `install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()` - Compilation
- `install_python_non_blocking()` - Dependencies (gguf, protobuf)

#### **Conversion Process**

1. **Merge LoRA to 16-bit** via `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
2. **Convert to GGUF** via `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` (HuggingFace format to GGUF)
3. **Quantize** via `llama-quantize` (GGUF to quantized GGUF)
4. **Optional Ollama Modelfile** creation via `create_ollama_modelfile()`

#### **Environment Handling**
- Kaggle: Disables CUDA, uses `install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)`
- Colab: Uses non-blocking installation
- Local: Standard installation

---

### 6. ADAPTER SAVING IMPLEMENTATION

**LoRA adapters** are saved via standard PEFT interface:
- Handled by `model.save_pretrained()` when `save_method="lora"`
- Stores adapter configuration in `adapter_config.json`
- Stores adapter weights in `adapter_model.bin`

**GGML conversion** for adapters:
- Uses llama.cpp's `convert-lora-to-ggml.py`
- Input: HuggingFace LoRA format
- Output: `ggml-adapter-model.bin` (binary format)

---

### 7. ONNX SUPPORT

**Status:** ❌ **NO ONNX SUPPORT FOUND**

Searched entire codebase - no ONNX-related code present. Not implemented.

---

### 8. KEY CLASS NAMES & METHOD SIGNATURES

#### **Public Methods Added to Models:**

| Method | Signature | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `save_pretrained_merged()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer=None, save_method="merged_16bit", push_to_hub=False, ...)` | Save merged model |
| `push_to_hub_merged()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer=None, save_method="merged_16bit", ...)` | Push merged to Hub |
| `save_pretrained_gguf()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Save as GGUF |
| `push_to_hub_gguf()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Push GGUF to Hub |
| `save_pretrained_torchao()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer=None, torchao_config=None, push_to_hub=False)` | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| `save_pretrained_ggml()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` | Save LoRA as GGML |
| `push_to_hub_ggml()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer, ...)` | Push LoRA GGML to Hub |
| `push_to_hub()` (wrapped) | Original signature + "Unsloth" tag | Enhanced HF push |

#### **Internal Helper Classes/Functions:**

| Name | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `_merge_lora()` | 159-187 | Merge LoRA weights with base |
| `fast_save_pickle()` | 190-200 | Optimized PyTorch saving |
| `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` | 116-134 | Detect sentencepiece tokenizers |
| `_free_cached_model()` | 137-156 | Clean HuggingFace cache |
| `get_executable()` | 937-950 | Find llama.cpp binaries |
| `try_execute()` | 814-836 | Execute shell commands with retry |

---

### 9. DATA FLOW DIAGRAM

```
User calls model.save_pretrained_merged()
    ↓
Patched via patch_saving_functions() using types.MethodType
    ↓
unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, ...) → unsloth_save_model(model=self, ...)
    ↓
Branches based on save_method:
    ├─ "lora" → Save PEFT adapters only (adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin)
    ├─ "merged_16bit" → Merge LoRA + save as float16 weights
    └─ "merged_4bit" → Merge LoRA + save as int4 weights

For GGUF:
    model.save_pretrained_gguf(...)
        ↓
    unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, ...)
        ↓
    1. Merge to 16-bit via unsloth_save_model()
    2. Install llama.cpp (if needed, non-blocking)
    3. Call save_to_gguf()
        ├─ convert-hf-to-gguf.py (HF → GGUF)
        └─ llama-quantize (GGUF → quantized GGUF)
    4. Create Ollama Modelfile
    5. Return file locations

For TorchAO:
    model.save_pretrained_torchao(...)
        ↓
    unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, ...)
        ↓
    1. Merge to 16-bit via unsloth_generic_save()
    2. Load merged model with TorchAoConfig
    3. Apply torchao.quantize_()
    4. Save to {save_directory}-torchao
```

---

### 10. SAVE METHOD CAPABILITIES MATRIX

| Feature | LoRA | Merged 16bit | Merged 4bit | GGUF | TorchAO | GGML |
|---------|------|--------------|------------|------|---------|------|
| Save locally | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Push to Hub | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Merge LoRA | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Quantization | N/A | N/A | bnb 4bit | llama.cpp | torchao | N/A |
| Inference ready | (adapter) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | (adapter) |
| Vision support | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Ollama support | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

### 11. FILE PATHS OF KEY IMPLEMENTATIONS

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| Main save/export | `/unsloth/save.py` |
| Model loading | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` |
| Model utilities | `/unsloth/models/loader_utils.py` |
| Tokenizer utils | `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` |
| LoRA kernels | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| External zoo utilities | Imported from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` |
| LLAMA.cpp integration | Imported from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp` |
| Tests (merged 16bit) | `/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py` |
| Tests (merged push) | `/tests/saving/language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py` |
| Tests (save methods) | `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` |

---

### 12. SUMMARY

The Unsloth save/export system is **highly modular and extensible**:

1. **Primary implementation** in `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)
2. **Three main save paradigms:**
   - **LoRA**: Save adapters only for inference with base model
   - **Merged**: Merge LoRA + base weights (16bit or 4bit quantization)
   - **GGUF**: Convert to llama.cpp quantized format with extensive quantization options
   - **TorchAO**: PyTorch-AO quantization for optimized inference
   - **GGML**: LoRA adapters in GGML format for llama.cpp

3. **Method registration** via monkey patching (`types.MethodType`) during model initialization
4. **No ONNX support** currently implemented
5. **External dependencies:** `unsloth_zoo` library handles low-level merging and llama.cpp integration
6. **Vision models** supported for merged/LoRA but NOT for GGUF conversion

```python
model.save_pretrained_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_save_locally, model)
model.push_to_hub_ggml = types.MethodType(unsloth_convert_lora_to_ggml_and_push_to_hub, model)
```

**Additionally:**
- Original `push_to_hub()` is wrapped with custom `unsloth_push_to_hub()` via exec()
- Stores original as `model.original_push_to_hub`
- Adds model tags automatically: `["unsloth"]`

#### **Initialization**

Called during model loading in `FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained()` and `FastModel.from_pretrained()` (from `models/loader.py`)

---

### 5. GGUF IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

#### **Quantization Constants** (Lines 79-107)
Defined dictionary `ALLOWED_QUANTS` with 20+ quantization methods

#### **Installation Flow**

Non-blocking installation functions:
- `install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()` - Git clone
- `install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()` - Compilation
- `install_python_non_blocking()` - Dependencies (gguf, protobuf)

#### **Conversion Process**

1. **Merge LoRA to 16-bit** via `unsloth_save_model()` with `save_method="merged_16bit"`
2. **Convert to GGUF** via `convert-hf-to-gguf.py` (HuggingFace format to GGUF)
3. **Quantize** via `llama-quantize` (GGUF to quantized GGUF)
4. **Optional Ollama Modelfile** creation via `create_ollama_modelfile()`

#### **Environment Handling**
- Kaggle: Disables CUDA, uses `install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda=False)`
- Colab: Uses non-blocking installation
- Local: Standard installation

---

### 6. ADAPTER SAVING IMPLEMENTATION

**LoRA adapters** are saved via standard PEFT interface:
- Handled by `model.save_pretrained()` when `save_method="lora"`
- Stores adapter configuration in `adapter_config.json`
- Stores adapter weights in `adapter_model.bin`

**GGML conversion** for adapters:
- Uses llama.cpp's `convert-lora-to-ggml.py`
- Input: HuggingFace LoRA format
- Output: `ggml-adapter-model.bin` (binary format)

---

### 7. ONNX SUPPORT

**Status:** ❌ **NO ONNX SUPPORT FOUND**

Searched entire codebase - no ONNX-related code present. Not implemented.

---

### 8. KEY CLASS NAMES & METHOD SIGNATURES

#### **Public Methods Added to Models:**

| Method | Signature | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| `save_pretrained_merged()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer=None, save_method="merged_16bit", push_to_hub=False, ...)` | Save merged model |
| `push_to_hub_merged()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer=None, save_method="merged_16bit", ...)` | Push merged to Hub |
| `save_pretrained_gguf()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Save as GGUF |
| `push_to_hub_gguf()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer, quantization_method="fast_quantized", ...)` | Push GGUF to Hub |
| `save_pretrained_torchao()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer=None, torchao_config=None, push_to_hub=False)` | Save with TorchAO quantization |
| `save_pretrained_ggml()` | `(save_directory, tokenizer, ...)` | Save LoRA as GGML |
| `push_to_hub_ggml()` | `(repo_id, tokenizer, ...)` | Push LoRA GGML to Hub |
| `push_to_hub()` (wrapped) | Original signature + "Unsloth" tag | Enhanced HF push |

#### **Internal Helper Classes/Functions:**

| Name | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `_merge_lora()` | 159-187 | Merge LoRA weights with base |
| `fast_save_pickle()` | 190-200 | Optimized PyTorch saving |
| `check_if_sentencepiece_model()` | 116-134 | Detect sentencepiece tokenizers |
| `_free_cached_model()` | 137-156 | Clean HuggingFace cache |
| `get_executable()` | 937-950 | Find llama.cpp binaries |
| `try_execute()` | 814-836 | Execute shell commands with retry |

---

### 9. DATA FLOW DIAGRAM

```
User calls model.save_pretrained_merged()
    ↓
Patched via patch_saving_functions() using types.MethodType
    ↓
unsloth_save_pretrained_merged(self, ...) → unsloth_save_model(model=self, ...)
    ↓
Branches based on save_method:
    ├─ "lora" → Save PEFT adapters only (adapter_config.json, adapter_model.bin)
    ├─ "merged_16bit" → Merge LoRA + save as float16 weights
    └─ "merged_4bit" → Merge LoRA + save as int4 weights

For GGUF:
    model.save_pretrained_gguf(...)
        ↓
    unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(self, ...)
        ↓
    1. Merge to 16-bit via unsloth_save_model()
    2. Install llama.cpp (if needed, non-blocking)
    3. Call save_to_gguf()
        ├─ convert-hf-to-gguf.py (HF → GGUF)
        └─ llama-quantize (GGUF → quantized GGUF)
    4. Create Ollama Modelfile
    5. Return file locations

For TorchAO:
    model.save_pretrained_torchao(...)
        ↓
    unsloth_save_pretrained_torchao(self, ...)
        ↓
    1. Merge to 16-bit via unsloth_generic_save()
    2. Load merged model with TorchAoConfig
    3. Apply torchao.quantize_()
    4. Save to {save_directory}-torchao
```

---

### 10. SAVE METHOD CAPABILITIES MATRIX

| Feature | LoRA | Merged 16bit | Merged 4bit | GGUF | TorchAO | GGML |
|---------|------|--------------|------------|------|---------|------|
| Save locally | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Push to Hub | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Merge LoRA | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Quantization | N/A | N/A | bnb 4bit | llama.cpp | torchao | N/A |
| Inference ready | (adapter) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | (adapter) |
| Vision support | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Ollama support | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ |

---

### 11. FILE PATHS OF KEY IMPLEMENTATIONS

| Component | File Path |
|-----------|-----------|
| Main save/export | `/unsloth/save.py` |
| Model loading | `/unsloth/models/loader.py` |
| Model utilities | `/unsloth/models/loader_utils.py` |
| Tokenizer utils | `/unsloth/tokenizer_utils.py` |
| LoRA kernels | `/unsloth/kernels/fast_lora.py` |
| External zoo utilities | Imported from `unsloth_zoo.saving_utils` |
| LLAMA.cpp integration | Imported from `unsloth_zoo.llama_cpp` |
| Tests (merged 16bit) | `/tests/saving/language_models/test_merge_model_perplexity_*.py` |
| Tests (merged push) | `/tests/saving/language_models/test_push_to_hub_merged.py` |
| Tests (save methods) | `/tests/saving/test_unsloth_save.py` |

---

### 12. SUMMARY

The Unsloth save/export system is **highly modular and extensible**:

1. **Primary implementation** in `unsloth/save.py` (2678 lines)
2. **Three main save paradigms:**
   - **LoRA**: Save adapters only for inference with base model
   - **Merged**: Merge LoRA + base weights (16bit or 4bit quantization)
   - **GGUF**: Convert to llama.cpp quantized format with extensive quantization options
   - **TorchAO**: PyTorch-AO quantization for optimized inference
   - **GGML**: LoRA adapters in GGML format for llama.cpp

3. **Method registration** via monkey patching (`types.MethodType`) during model initialization
4. **No ONNX support** currently implemented
5. **External dependencies:** `unsloth_zoo` library handles low-level merging and llama.cpp integration
6. **Vision models** supported for merged/LoRA but NOT for GGUF conversion

1768	def unsloth_save_pretrained_gguf(
1769	    self,
1770	    save_directory       : Union[str, os.PathLike],
1771	    tokenizer            = None,

1788	    """
1789	        Same as .save_pretrained(...) except 4bit weights are auto
1790	        converted to float16 then converted to GGUF / llama.cpp format.
1791	
1792	        Choose for `quantization_method` to be:
1793	        "not_quantized"  : "Recommended. Fast conversion. Slow inference, big files.",
1794	        "fast_quantized" : "Recommended. Fast conversion. OK inference, OK file size.",
1795	        "quantized"      : "Recommended. Slow conversion. Fast inference, small files.",
1796	        "f32"     : "Not recommended. Retains 100% accuracy, but super slow and memory hungry.",
1797	        "f16"     : "Fastest conversion + retains 100% accuracy. Slow and memory hungry.",
1798	        "q8_0"    : "Fast conversion. High resource use, but generally acceptable.",
1799	        "q4_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q4_K",
1800	        "q5_k_m"  : "Recommended. Uses Q6_K for half of the attention.wv and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q5_K",
1801	        "q2_k"    : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.vw and feed_forward.w2 tensors, Q2_K for the other tensors.",
1802	        "q3_k_l"  : "Uses Q5_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1803	        "q3_k_m"  : "Uses Q4_K for the attention.wv, attention.wo, and feed_forward.w2 tensors, else Q3_K",
1804	        "q3_k_s"  : "Uses Q3_K for all tensors",
1805	        "q4_0"    : "Original quant method, 4-bit.",
1806	        "q4_1"    : "Higher accuracy than q4_0 but not as high as q5_0. However has quicker inference than q5 models.",
1807	        "q4_k_s"  : "Uses Q4_K for all tensors",
1808	        "q4_k"    : "alias for q4_k_m",
1809	        "q5_k"    : "alias for q5_k_m",
1810	        "q5_0"    : "Higher accuracy, higher resource usage and slower inference.",
1811	        "q5_1"    : "Even higher accuracy, resource usage and slower inference.",
1812	        "q5_k_s"  : "Uses Q5_K for all tensors",
1813	        "q6_k"    : "Uses Q8_K for all tensors",
1814	        "iq2_xxs" : "2.06 bpw quantization",
1815	        "iq2_xs"  : "2.31 bpw quantization",
1816	        "iq3_xxs" : "3.06 bpw quantization",
1817	        "q3_k_xs" : "3-bit extra small quantization",
1818	    """
1819	    if tokenizer is None:
1820	        raise ValueError("Unsloth: Saving to GGUF must have a tokenizer.")
1821	
1822	    arguments = dict(locals())
1823	    arguments["model"]        = self
1824	    arguments["tokenizer"]    = tokenizer
1825	    arguments["push_to_hub"]  = False # We save ourselves
1826	    arguments["save_method"] = "merged_16bit" # Must be 16bit
1827	    del arguments["self"]
1828	    del arguments["quantization_method"]
1829	    del arguments["first_conversion"]
1830

1833	
1834	    # Non blocking install GGUF first
1835	    if not os.path.exists("llama.cpp"):

1838	            # Kaggle is weird - no blocking installs, and no CUDA?
1839	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1840	            python_install.wait()
1841	            install_llama_cpp_blocking(use_cuda = False)
1842	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)
1843	            makefile = None
1844	        else:
1845	            git_clone = install_llama_cpp_clone_non_blocking()
1846	            python_install = install_python_non_blocking(["gguf", "protobuf"])
1847	            git_clone.wait()
1848	            makefile = install_llama_cpp_make_non_blocking()
1849	            new_save_directory, old_username = unsloth_save_model(**arguments)

2514	      `torchao_config` (TorchAOBaseConfig): configuration for torchao quantization, full list: https://docs.pytorch.org/ao/main/api_ref_quantization.html#inference-apis-for-quantize

2523	    del arguments["self"]

2525	    unsloth_generic_save(**arguments)

2529	    from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer, TorchAoConfig
2530	    from torchao import quantize_
2531	    if torchao_config is None:

2540	        kwargs = {"dtype" : "auto"}
2541	    model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(

2543	        device_map = "auto",
2544	        quantization_config = quantization_config,
2545	        **kwargs,
2546	    )
2547	
2548	    torchao_save_directory = save_directory + "-torchao"
2549

65	def calculate_settings(n : int) -> (int, int,):
66	    BLOCK_SIZE : int = next_power_of_2(n)
67	    if BLOCK_SIZE > MAX_FUSED_SIZE:
68	        raise RuntimeError(f"Cannot launch Triton kernel since n = {n} exceeds "\
69	                           f"the maximum CUDA blocksize = {MAX_FUSED_SIZE}.")
70	    num_warps : int = 4
71	    if   BLOCK_SIZE >= 32768: num_warps = 32
72	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  8192: num_warps = 16
73	    elif BLOCK_SIZE >=  2048: num_warps = 8
74	    return BLOCK_SIZE, num_warps

188	def QUANT_STATE(W): return getattr(W, "quant_state", None)

190	def get_lora_parameters(proj):
191	    """
192	    Return a 5-tuple of (weight, weight quant_state, lora A, lora B, and lora scale).
193	    If QAT is enabled, additionally fake quantize the base layer and lora weights.
194	    """
195	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
196	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
197	    W = base_layer.weight
198	
199	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to base layer weights for QAT
200	    weight_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
201	    if weight_fake_quantizer is not None:
202	        W = weight_fake_quantizer(W)
203	
204	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
205	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
206	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None
207	    pass
208	
209	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
210	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
211	    adapter = adapter[0]
212	
213	    # Optionally apply fake quantization to lora weights for QAT
214	    lora_A_linear = proj.lora_A[adapter]
215	    lora_B_linear = proj.lora_B[adapter]
216	    lora_A_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_A_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
217	    lora_B_fake_quantizer = getattr(lora_B_linear, "weight_fake_quantizer", None)
218	    A = lora_A_linear.weight
219	    B = lora_B_linear.weight
220	    if lora_A_fake_quantizer is not None:
221	        A = lora_A_fake_quantizer(A)
222	    if lora_B_fake_quantizer is not None:
223	        B = lora_B_fake_quantizer(B)
224	
225	    return (
226	        W,
227	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
228	        A,
229	        B,
230	        proj.scaling[adapter],
231	    )
232	pass
233

235	def get_lora_parameters_bias(proj):
236	    # For DPO or disabled adapters
237	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj) # (proj.base_layer if hasattr(proj, "base_layer") else proj)
238	    W = base_layer.weight
239	
240	    # if not hasattr(proj, "disable_adapters") or proj.disable_adapters or proj.merged:
241	    if getattr(proj, "disable_adapters", True) or proj.merged:
242	        return W, getattr(W, "quant_state", None), None, None, None, base_layer.bias
243	    pass
244	
245	    adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapters", None)
246	    if adapter is None: adapter = getattr(proj, "active_adapter", ("default"))
247	    adapter = adapter[0]
248	
249	    return (
250	        W,
251	        getattr(W, "quant_state", None),
252	        proj.lora_A [adapter].weight,
253	        proj.lora_B [adapter].weight,
254	        proj.scaling[adapter],
255	        base_layer.bias,
256	    )
257	pass

260	def _maybe_fake_quantize_activations(X: torch.Tensor, proj: torch.nn.Module) -> torch.Tensor:
261	    """
262	    If QAT is enabled, fake quantize the input activations.
263	    Otherwise, just return the input activations as is.
264	    Weights are fake quantized separately in `get_lora_parameters`.
265	    """
266	    base_layer = getattr(proj, "base_layer", proj)
267	    activation_fake_quantizer = getattr(base_layer, "activation_fake_quantizer", None)
268	    if activation_fake_quantizer is not None:
269	        X = activation_fake_quantizer(X)
270	    return X

275	if DEVICE_TYPE == "xpu" and HAS_XPU_STREAM:
276	    @torch.inference_mode
277	    def fast_dequantize(W, quant_state = None, out = None, use_global_buffer = False):
278	        # TODO: After adding XPU BNB support, check this function
279	        if quant_state is None: return W
280	        if type(quant_state) is not list:
281	            # New quant_state as a class
282	            # https://github.com/TimDettmers/bitsandbytes/pull/763/files
283	            absmax     = quant_state.absmax
284	            shape      = quant_state.shape
285	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
286	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
287	            offset     = quant_state.offset
288	            state2     = quant_state.state2
289	            absmax2    = state2.absmax
290	            code2      = state2.code
291	            blocksize2 = state2.blocksize
292	        else:
293	            # Old quant_state as a list of lists
294	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, _, _ = quant_state
295	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
296	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
297	        pass
298	        global XPU_STREAMS
299	        device = W.device
300	        device_index = device.index
301	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
302	
303	        n_elements_absmax = absmax.numel()
304	        # Create weight matrix
305	        if use_global_buffer:
306	
307	            # Use same buffers for faster inference
308	            size = shape[0]*shape[1]
309	            global WEIGHT_BUFFERS
310	            global ABSMAX_BUFFERS
311	            WEIGHT_BUFFER = WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index]
312	            ABSMAX_BUFFER = ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index]
313	            if WEIGHT_BUFFER is None:
314	                WEIGHT_BUFFERS[device_index] = WEIGHT_BUFFER = torch_empty(size, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
315	                ABSMAX_BUFFERS[device_index] = ABSMAX_BUFFER = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch.float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
316	
317	            if size > WEIGHT_BUFFER.numel(): WEIGHT_BUFFER.resize_(size)
318	            if n_elements_absmax > ABSMAX_BUFFER.numel(): ABSMAX_BUFFER.resize_(n_elements_absmax)
319	
320	            out = WEIGHT_BUFFER[:size].view(shape)
321	            out_absmax = ABSMAX_BUFFER[:n_elements_absmax]
322	        else:
323	            if out is None:
324	                out = torch_empty(shape, dtype = dtype, device = device, requires_grad = False)
325	            else:
326	                assert(out.shape == shape)
327	                assert(out.dtype == dtype)
328	            out_absmax = torch_empty(n_elements_absmax, dtype = torch_float32, device = device, requires_grad = False)
329	        pass
330	
331	        # NF4 dequantization of statistics
332	        ptr_out_absmax = get_ptr(out_absmax)
333	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
334	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
335	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), ptr_out_absmax,
336	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(n_elements_absmax), XPU_STREAM
337	            )
338	            out_absmax += offset
339	
340	            # Dequantize W
341	            fx = cdequantize_blockwise_fp16_nf4 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
342	                 cdequantize_blockwise_bf16_nf4
343	            fx(get_ptr(None), get_ptr(W), ptr_out_absmax, get_ptr(out),
344	               ctypes_c_int(blocksize), ctypes_c_int(out.numel()), XPU_STREAM,)
345	        pass
346	        # Careful returning transposed data
347	        is_transposed = (True if W.shape[0] == 1 else False)
348	        return out.t() if is_transposed else out
349	    pass

492	            shape      = quant_state.shape
493	            dtype      = quant_state.dtype
494	            blocksize  = quant_state.blocksize
495	            stats      = quant_state.code
496	            offset     = quant_state.offset
497	            state2     = quant_state.state2
498	            absmax2    = state2.absmax

501	        else:
502	            absmax, shape, dtype, blocksize, compressed_stats, quant_type, stats = quant_state
503	            offset, state2 = compressed_stats
504	            absmax2, code2, blocksize2, _, _, _, _ = state2
505	        pass
506	        global XPU_STREAMS
507	        device = W.device
508	        device_index = device.index
509	        XPU_STREAM = XPU_STREAMS[device_index]
510	
511	        # assert(dtype == X.dtype)
512	        bout = shape[0]
513	
514	        if out is None:
515	            out = torch_empty((1, 1, bout,), dtype = dtype, device = device)
516	        # else:
517	        #     assert(out.shape == (1, 1, bout,))
518	        # pass
519	
520	        n = 1
521	        m = shape[0]
522	        k = shape[1]
523	        lda = shape[0]
524	        ldc = shape[0]
525	        ldb = (hd+1)//2
526	        m = ctypes_c_int32(m)
527	        n = ctypes_c_int32(n)
528	        k = ctypes_c_int32(k)
529	        lda = ctypes_c_int32(lda)
530	        ldb = ctypes_c_int32(ldb)
531	        ldc = ctypes_c_int32(ldc)
532	
533	        df = torch_empty(absmax.shape, dtype = torch_float32, device = device)
534	        with torch_gpu_device(device):
535	            cdequantize_blockwise_fp32(
536	                get_ptr(code2), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(absmax2), get_ptr(df),
537	                ctypes_c_int(blocksize2), ctypes_c_int(df.numel()), XPU_STREAM,
538	            )
539	            df += offset
540	            absmax = df
541	
542	            fx = cgemm_4bit_inference_naive_fp16 if dtype == torch_float16 else \
543	                cgemm_4bit_inference_naive_bf16
544	
545	            blocksize = ctypes_c_int32(blocksize)
546	            fx(m, n, k, get_ptr(X), get_ptr(W), get_ptr(absmax), get_ptr(stats), get_ptr(out),
547	               lda, ldb, ldc, blocksize, XPU_STREAM,)
548	        pass
549	
550	        return out

691	def fast_linear_forward(proj, X, temp_lora = None, out = None):
692	
693	    W, W_quant, lora_A, lora_B, lora_S, bias = get_lora_parameters_bias(proj)
694	    bsz, q_len, in_dim = X.shape
695	    if q_len != 1: return matmul_lora(X, W, W_quant, lora_A, lora_B, lora_S)
696	
697	    if W_quant is None:
698	        out = torch_matmul(X, W.t(), out = out)
699	    elif bsz == 1 and q_len == 1:
700	        out = fast_gemv(X, W, W_quant, out = out)
701	    else:
702	        W = fast_dequantize(W.t(), W_quant, use_global_buffer = True)
703	        out = torch_matmul(X, W, out = out)
704	    pass
705
